claims.source_quote: Agriculture is the lifeline of Pakistan’s economy accounting for 19.5 percent of the gross domestic product, employing 42.3 percent
claims.claim: Agriculture was described as central to Pakistan's economy, contributing 19.5 percent of GDP, employing 42.3 percent of the labour force, and supplying raw material for value-added sectors.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Agriculture contribution to GDP and employment

claims.source_quote: demand for high-value perishable products such as fruits, vegetables, dairy, and meat is rising
claims.claim: Rapid urban growth was raising demand for high-value perishable agricultural products such as fruits, vegetables, dairy, and meat.
claims.cause: rapid growth of Pakistan’s urban areas
claims.effect: rising demand for high-value perishable products

claims.source_quote: Government is focusing to increase the yield for rural growers through major infrastructure investments
claims.claim: The government was focusing on increasing yields for rural growers through major infrastructure investments, including reliable transport networks and modern supply-chain building blocks.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Rural grower yields and supply chains
claims.cause: major infrastructure investments including reliable transport networks
claims.effect: increase the yield for rural growers

claims.source_quote: CPEC will go a long way in the enhancement of agribusiness benefits
claims.claim: CPEC was expected to enhance agribusiness benefits by supporting value-added product innovation and supply chains.
claims.cause: tapping value-added product innovation and supply chain

claims.source_quote: achieved growth of 3.46 percent against the target of 3.5 percent and last year’s meager growth of 0.27 percent
claims.claim: Agriculture sector growth in 2016-17 was 3.46 percent, nearly meeting the 3.5 percent target and improving sharply from 0.27 percent growth in the previous year.

comparison.baseline: 2015-16 growth of 0.27 percent and 2016-17 target of 3.5 percent

claims.source_quote: possible by better harvesting of major crops through greater availability of agriculture inputs like water, agriculture credit and intensive fertilizers offtake
claims.claim: Better harvesting of major crops was attributed to greater availability of agricultural inputs such as water, agricultural credit, and intensive fertilizer offtake.
claims.cause: greater availability of agriculture inputs like water, agriculture credit and intensive fertilizers offtake

claims.source_quote: growth in crops was registered at 3.02 percent against the negative growth of 4.97 percent during the same period last year
claims.claim: Crop growth recovered to 3.02 percent in 2016-17 from negative growth of 4.97 percent in the same period last year.

claims.source_quote: The upturn in crop yields by significant margins occurred both on account of increase in production and improved yields.
claims.claim: The upturn in crop yields was attributed to both increased production and improved yields.
claims.cause: increase in production and improved yields
claims.effect: upturn in crop yields by significant margins

claims.source_quote: Other crops accounted for 11.03 percent in value addition of agriculture grew by 0.21 percent during 201617
claims.claim: Other crops grew by 0.21 percent in 2016-17, slower than 0.59 percent in the previous year, due to declines in vegetables and oilseeds production.
claims.constraint_or_risk: decline in the production of vegetables and oilseeds
claims.cause: decline in the production of vegetables and oilseeds
claims.effect: other crops grew by 0.21 percent during 2016-17 against 0.59 percent during the same period last year

claims.source_quote: Forestry sector having contribution of 2.33 percent in the agriculture value addition witnessed a significant growth of 14.49 percent
claims.claim: Forestry, contributing 2.33 percent of agricultural value addition, grew significantly by 14.49 percent in 2016-17 due to higher timber production reported by Khyber Pakhtunkhwa.
claims.cause: higher timber production reported by Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
claims.effect: Forestry sector witnessed significant growth of 14.49 percent

claims.source_quote: P: Provisional
claims.claim: The 2016-17 agriculture growth figures in Table 2.1 were marked provisional and used base year 2005-06.

claims.source_quote: During  2016-17, the availability of water for Kharif 2016 stood at 71.4 million acre feet (MAF)
claims.claim: Kharif 2016 water availability increased to 71.4 MAF, exceeding both Kharif 2015 and normal supplies, while Rabi 2016-17 water availability fell below both Rabi 2015-16 and normal availability.

claims.source_quote: Livestock contributes 58.33 percent to agricultural value addition and 11.39 percent to GDP.
claims.claim: Livestock was the largest listed contributor to agricultural value addition, contributing 58.33 percent to agricultural value addition and 11.39 percent to GDP.

comparison.baseline: important crops, other crops, forestry, and fishing shares

claims.source_quote: Cotton production was estimated at 10.671 million b **a** les registering 7.6 percent increase
claims.claim: Cotton production in 2016-17 was estimated at 10.671 million bales, up 7.6 percent from 2015-16 but substantially below the 14.1 million bale target.
claims.constraint_or_risk: production lower than the target by a considerable margin

comparison.baseline: 2015-16 production of 9.917 million bales and target of 14.1 million bales

claims.source_quote: weaker than expected recovery in cotton production is mainly due to **a** sharp decline of 14.2 percent in the sowing a **r** ea
claims.claim: The weaker-than-expected recovery in cotton production was mainly attributed to a sharp decline in sowing area, following previous pest losses and low domestic prices that shifted growers toward sugarcane and maize.
claims.constraint_or_risk: previous year’s pest infestation and low domestic prices at the sowing time
claims.cause: sharp decline of 14.2 percent in the sowing area due to previous pest infestation and low domestic prices
claims.effect: weaker than expected recovery in cotton production and growers shifted from cotton to sugarcane and maize

claims.source_quote: Table 2.5: Production and Consumption of Major Cotton Growing Countries
claims.claim: Table 2.5 presents estimated and projected production and consumption data for major cotton-growing countries, with 2016-17 marked projected.
claims.actor: Pakistan Central Cotton Committee, Ministry of Textile Industry
claims.geography: India; China; USA; Pakistan; Brazil; Uzbekistan; Europe & Turkey; Vietnam; Bangladesh; World

claims.source_quote: production of sugarcane portrayed a very promising p **i** cture and reached to historical high of 73. **6** million tonnes
claims.claim: Sugarcane production reached a historical high of 73.6 million tonnes in 2016-17, increasing 12.4 percent over 2015-16 and exceeding the target by 9.0 percent.

comparison.baseline: 2015-16 production of 65.5 million tonnes and target of 67.5 million tonnes

claims.source_quote: The production increased d **u** e to increase in area cultivated as it shi **f** ted from other competitive crops
claims.claim: Sugarcane production increased because cultivated area expanded as land shifted from other distressed competitive crops, including cotton affected by pest attacks.
claims.constraint_or_risk: frequent distress in other competitive crops and pest attacks on cotton
claims.cause: increase in area cultivated as it shifted from other competitive crops facing frequent distress

comparison.baseline: last year’s area of 1131 thousand hectares

claims.source_quote: Rice production stoo **d** at 6849 thousand tonnes achieving its targeted **p** roduction of 6838 thousand tonnes
claims.claim: Rice production in 2016-17 stood at 6,849 thousand tonnes, achieving its target and increasing 0.7 percent over the previous year, despite a slight decline in sown area.

comparison.baseline: 2015-16 production of 6801 thousand tonnes and target of 6838 thousand tonnes

claims.source_quote: Rice area decreased due to decline in do **m** estic prices of rice
claims.claim: Rice area decreased because lower domestic rice prices reduced area under the crop and shifted growers to sugarcane and maize.
claims.constraint_or_risk: decline in domestic prices of rice
claims.cause: decline in domestic prices of rice
claims.effect: reduced area under rice and growers shifted to sugarcane and maize

comparison.baseline: last year’s area of 2793 thousand hectares

claims.source_quote: Wheat production was estimated at 25.750 million tonnes **d** uring 2016-17, witnessing an increase of 0. **5** percent
claims.claim: Wheat production in 2016-17 was estimated at 25.750 million tonnes, increasing 0.5 percent from the previous year despite a 1.9 percent decrease in sown area.

comparison.baseline: 2015-16 production of 25.633 million tonnes and area of 9224 thousand hectares
comparison.comparator: 2016-17 production of 25.750 million tonnes and area of 9052 thousand hectares

claims.source_quote: The production increased du **e** to better supply of inputs which contributed in enhancing per hectare yield.
claims.claim: Wheat production increased because better input supply helped raise per hectare yield.
claims.effect: enhancing per hectare yield and increased production

claims.source_quote: Maize crop producti **o** n stood at record high of 6.130 million tonne **s** during 2016-17
claims.claim: Maize production reached a record high of 6.130 million tonnes in 2016-17, increasing 16.3 percent over the previous year as area under maize also expanded.

comparison.baseline: 2015-16 production of 5.271 million tonnes and area of 1191 thousand hectares
comparison.comparator: 2016-17 production of 6.130 million tonnes and area of 1334 thousand hectares

claims.source_quote: record prod **u** ction has been achieved on the back of inten **s** ive use of critical agricultural inputs
claims.claim: Record maize production was attributed to intensive use of critical agricultural inputs, especially fertilizers, together with increased area sown.
claims.cause: intensive use of critical agricultural inputs (fertilizers) coupled with increase in area sown

claims.source_quote: Gram is the largest Rabi puls **e** crop, accounting for 76 percent of total production of pulses
claims.claim: Gram was the largest Rabi pulse crop, accounting for 76 percent of pulse production and registering a 25.5 percent production increase in 2016-17.

claims.source_quote: Barley, Jowar, Rapeseed & Mastard and Tobacco h **a** s witnessed  decrease in production
claims.claim: Barley, Jowar, Rapeseed & Mustard, and Tobacco all saw production decreases in 2016-17 due to decreased area sown.

claims.source_quote: production of Moong, Onion and Chillies recorded positive growth of 27.4 percent, 2.7 percent and 0.2 percent
claims.claim: Moong, Onion, and Chillies recorded positive production growth in 2016-17 compared with the previous year, with the increase attributed to expanded cultivated area.
claims.effect: positive growth in production of Moong, Onion and Chillies

claims.source_quote: production of pulses Masoor (Lentil), Mash and Potato decreased by 17.9 percent 15.3 percent and 3.2 percent
claims.claim: Masoor, Mash, and Potato production decreased in 2016-17 compared with the previous year.

claims.source_quote: local production contributed 0.462 million tonnes (14 percent) and the import share of edible oil/oilseeds was 3.264 million tonnes (86 percent)
claims.claim: Pakistan's edible oil availability in 2015-16 relied heavily on imports, with local production contributing 14 percent and imports contributing 86 percent.
claims.constraint_or_risk: high import dependence for edible oil/oilseeds

claims.source_quote: 1.98 million tonnes edible oil of value Rs.152.514 billion (US$ 1.457 billion) was imported
claims.claim: During July-March 2016-17, edible oil imports were 1.98 million tonnes valued at Rs.152.514 billion, lower by 4.0 percent than the same period last year.

claims.source_quote: Local production of edible oil during 2016-17 (July-March) is provisionally estimated at 0.446 million tonnes.
claims.claim: Local edible oil production and total edible oil availability for July-March 2016-17 were reported as provisional estimates.

claims.source_quote:
Source: Pakistan Oilseed Development Board (PODB), Pakistan Bureau of Statistics
P: Provisional
claims.claim: Table 2.12 reports provisional July-March 2016-17 area and production figures for major oilseed crops from PODB and Pakistan Bureau of Statistics.
claims.indicator_or_subject: Major oilseed crops area and production
claims.actor: Pakistan Oilseed Development Board (PODB), Pakistan Bureau of Statistics
